markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Add stable data-testid/selector IDs and debug payloads for all important editor controls and states. This MT belongs to TiptapMonacoIntegration. Integrate Tiptap and Monaco as the full-featured Handshake editor surface.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Tiptap/ProseMirror is the canonical rich document engine for this WP; Monaco is the canonical code-block/code editing surface embedded inside Handshake.
Tiptap and Monaco must be fully integrated into Handshake packaging, UI state, backend save/load, visual debugging, and tests; no separate external editor app may be required.
scope.expected_behavior: EditorVisualDebugSelectors leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: EditorVisualDebugSelectors is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified 5d4da13a: visual_debug.test.ts 3/3 + RichTextEditor.test.tsx 6/6 - stable visual-debug selectors + __HS_EDITOR_DEBUG__ payload (lint 7c10517d). TiptapMonacoIntegration group complete: full app vitest 60 files/442 tests green; tsc --noEmit + eslint clean; offline Playwright roundtrip PASS (bundled Monaco mounts + round-trips, ZERO external network requests, worker chunks loopback-only). RichTextEditor wired into RichDocumentView over the real rich-doc backend; no src/backend or .GOV product code touched.

evidence.finding: M15 unconditional O(doc) debug walk + double re-render per keystroke; L19 last-writer-wins debug key
evidence.fix: isEditorDebugEnabled() gating (explicit __HS_EDITOR_DEBUG_ENABLE__ override; default OFF in production bundles, harnesses opt in); transaction+selectionUpdate refresh coalesced to one microtask; per-editor __HS_EDITOR_DEBUG_BY_ID__[debugId] namespace (RichDocumentView passes rich_document_id), legacy global kept for back-compat
tests: visual_debug.test.ts: explicit enable/disable overrides; defaults ON outside production builds
visual_debug.test.ts: publishes on both the last-writer global and the per-id namespace
playwright dependency lane 13/13 green against the production-built harness (explicit opt-in path proven)

adversarial_review_v2.suites_observed: visual_debug.test.ts 3/3 + RichTextEditor.test.tsx 6/6 PASS; playwright reads __HS_EDITOR_DEBUG__ successfully

findings.title: Unconditional O(doc) debug snapshot on every transaction AND selection update in production
findings.evidence: RichTextEditor.tsx:166-206 calls buildEditorDebugSnapshot (full doc.descendants walk, visual_debug.ts:60-92) from BOTH the transaction and selectionUpdate handlers, plus forceRefresh re-rendering the component each event - per keystroke the document is walked twice and the React tree re-renders twice, with no debug-mode gate. Compounds with the echo-loop getJSON+setContent cost (MT-171) into structurally quadratic typing on large documents.
findings.fix: Gate snapshot publication behind a debug flag (env/window toggle) or debounce to idle (requestIdleCallback/150ms); keep data-* attrs cheap (counts from nodeCounts cache, not fresh walks).

findings.title: Single global __HS_EDITOR_DEBUG__ key: last-writer-wins across multiple mounted editors
findings.evidence: visual_debug.ts:95 one global key; RichTextEditor.tsx:170 writes unconditionally - with two documents open (future split view, MT-244 panes, or harness + app), a no-context model cannot attribute the snapshot to a document (GLOBAL-BUILD parallel-attribution intent).
findings.fix: Namespace by document/editor id: __HS_EDITOR_DEBUG__[docId] with a lastUpdated field; expose the id on the rich-text-editor root data-* for correlation.

parity_gaps: ED-DBG-001 visual debug selectors: PRESENT (selector set + payload proven in playwright)
missing_tests: debug publication is gated/debounced (perf regression guard once fixed)
two mounted editors publish attributable snapshots

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-172 requires stable data-testid selectors plus a machine-readable visual-debug payload so a no-context model / visual lane can assert editor state without screen-scraping. The code delivers exactly this: a real Tiptap-walking debug snapshot published on stable globals + reflected on stable selectors, wired into the real integrated editor and the real rich-doc-backed document view, with proof from a real built-harness Playwright run against bundled Monaco.
findings: visual_debug.ts:60-92 buildEditorDebugSnapshot walks the real ProseMirror/Tiptap doc (node counts, monacoCodeBlock language+contentHash+codeLength, hsLink refKind/refValue/resolved, selection, editable) - a concrete machine-readable payload, not a stub.

RichTextEditor.tsx:432-438 + 487-526 publish the snapshot (gated by isEditorDebugEnabled(), coalesced into one microtask) on __HS_EDITOR_DEBUG__ and per-id __HS_EDITOR_DEBUG_BY_ID__[debugId]; lines 905-907 reflect counts on stable data-* selectors (data-code-block-count/-link-count/-selection-empty). EDITOR_STABLE_SELECTORS (visual_debug.ts:151-169) documents the canonical selector set.

Real wiring: RichDocumentView.tsx:901,938 mounts the real RichTextEditor over the real rich-doc backend and passes debugId={doc.rich_document_id} - the selectors/payload exist on the production document surface, not just in tests.

Sub-rule 2 proof is real, not mock-only: RichTextEditor.test.tsx mounts the REAL integrated Tiptap editor (new Editor with buildHandshakeEditorExtensions) and asserts live data-code-block-count/data-link-count + __HS_EDITOR_DEBUG__.nodeCounts; tests/dependency_policy/rich_editor_typing.spec.ts drives the BUILT dist-harness in a real browser with bundled Monaco, reading __HS_EDITOR_DEBUG__ selection/codeBlocks and asserting ZERO external network requests (bootEditor aborts any non-loopback URL).

Sub-rule 1: placeholder/stub grep over the impl returned only legitimate HTML input placeholder= attributes and comments noting surfaces are 'no longer a hidden stub' - no todo!/unimplemented!/'deferred to follow-on'/*Unavailable escape reachable from the required behavior.

Prior adversarial findings (M15 unconditional O(doc) walk per transaction; L19 last-writer-wins single global key) are genuinely fixed in commit 701904be via isEditorDebugEnabled() gating, microtask-coalesced refresh, and the per-id namespace, each with added tests (visual_debug.test.ts:84-111).

Boundary/scope clean: both commits (5d4da13a, 701904be) touch only app/src and tests; no /.GOV/, no src/backend, no SQLite/Docker/external-search/CDN-worker violations. Durable-state authority is not owned by this UI/debug MT, so no PostgreSQL row is required.
Aside (non-blocking): MT contract still carries lifecycle.status READY_FOR_VALIDATION with claimed_by/completed_by null - paperwork state, not a code defect.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
